title: [phase:critique] [family:backend-fit] [provider_hint:codex] Review suitability of the Google AI agents technical guidance for using Google agent tooling as the backend layer for Cursorvers.Inc. Focus on risks, lock-in, security, and what should not be delegated to agents. Findings first.
summary_text: **Findings (highest risk first)** 1. **Critical: Accountability is not delegable to the platform.** Google terms explicitly put responsibility for AI-agent actions, fit-for-use, access authorization, and supervision on the customer; agent actions are also treated separately from “Generated Output.” 2. **Critical: Key security features are still pre-GA/preview in places.** Agent identity and multiple threat detections are preview, and Pre-GA terms exclude SLA/indemnity and can exclude data-processing commitments. 3. **Critical: “Zero retention” is conditional, not automatic.** Vertex docs state prompt/context/output retention for some grounding paths (notably Google Search/Maps grounding) and no disable switch for some modes; this is a hard compliance boundary. 4. **High: VPC-SC sequencing foot-gun.** If an agent is deployed before perimeter enrollment, docs state it may remain outside VPC-SC protection with internet access. 5. **High: Access control is coarse by default.** Sessions/Memory are project-level IAM by default, and `ListSessions` lacks IAM-condition support, forcing broader permissions than ideal in multi-tenant setups. 6. **High: Backend reliability requires quota engineering up front.** Default Agent Engine quotas are low for backend workloads (for example query QPM), and Google calls out likely `429` without proactive increases. 7. **High: Lock-in risk is real despite open standards.** ADK/A2A/MCP reduce portability risk, but core runtime features (Sessions, Memory Bank, IAM attributes, SCC integration) are GCP-coupled. Also, AI/ML terms restrict competitive-use patterns. 8. **Medium: Security observability has tradeoffs.** Some threat detections are preview; VPC-SC mode can remove request/response logging, which affects forensics and debugging depth. 9. **Medium: Platform/API churn risk.** Agent Engine SDK refactoring + renamed/legacy resource semantics indicate evolution; this is manageable but requires strict version pinning and compatibility tests. **Suitability verdict for Cursorvers.Inc** - **Fit:** as a **supervised agent runtime layer** for assistive/orchestrative tasks with strict policy gates. - **Not fit:** as a **fully autonomous backend of record** for high-impact operations. **What should not be delegated to agents** 1. Final approval/execution of financial transfers, purchases, refunds, or credits. 2. IAM/policy/perimeter changes (roles, keys, VPC-SC, firewall, org-policy). 3. Destructive data operations (delete/export/mass update) without human approval. 4. Compliance/legal determinations and policy-signoff decisions. 5. Production release approvals and incident closure decisions. 6. Any workflow that requires guaranteed zero-retention unless the exact Google feature path is contractually and technically validated. **Mandatory callout (process)** - This is a **single Codex lane** critique. Under your orchestration policy, final adjudication should include independent non-Codex lanes (Claude + GLM or documented fallback quorum). **Sources** - https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/agent-engine/overview - https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/quotas - https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/vertex-ai-zero-data-retention - https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/agent-engine/private-service-connect-interface - https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/vpc-service-controls - https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/agent-engine/agent-identity - https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/agent-engine/sessions/iam-conditions - https://docs.cloud.google.com/agent-builder/agent-engine/memory-bank/iam-conditions - https://docs.cloud.google.com/security-command-center/docs/agent-engine-threat-detection-overview - https://google.github.io/adk-docs/safety/ - https://google.github.io/adk-docs/mcp/ - https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/ - https://github.com/google/adk-python - https://cloud.google.com/legal/archive/terms/service-terms/index-20251120
